isolation.database: :memory: (in-memory SQLite for tests), temp file for WAL test

VAL-DB-001.details: get_conn() on a file-based temp DB returns journal_mode=wal. Note: in-memory DBs return 'memory' which is expected SQLite behavior — WAL is only applicable to file-based databases. Tested with a temp file DB to confirm WAL is set correctly.

VAL-DB-002.details: get_conn() returns busy_timeout=5000 as confirmed by PRAGMA busy_timeout query on both in-memory and file-based databases.

VAL-DB-003.details: Inserted 3 test messages into in-memory DB with schema. search_fts('webpack') returned 2 matching results (user question and assistant response containing 'webpack'). FTS5 keyword matching works correctly.

VAL-DB-004.details: Inserted 2 test facts into in-memory DB. search_facts_fts('webpack') returned 1 matching result with correct fact text and confidence. FTS5 keyword matching on facts works correctly.

VAL-DB-005.details: Tested full session ID match ('sess-001' returned 2 messages) and prefix match ('sess-00' returned all 3 messages from both sessions). Prefix matching uses LIKE query for IDs shorter than 36 characters.

VAL-DB-006.details: list_recent_sessions() returned 2 session records with all required fields: session_id, project, msg_count, first_msg, last_msg. Sessions ordered by last_msg DESC.

VAL-DB-007.details: store_fact() inserted a fact with all fields (fact, category, confidence, project, session_id, source_message_id, timestamp, last_validated). Retrieved via SQL and verified all fields match. Also verified that last_validated=None when not provided.

VAL-DB-008.details: grep for 'sqlite3.connect(' across all .py files and bin/claude-recall found exactly one match: src/memory_db.py line 56. Zero hits in any other file (mcp_server.py, inject.py, curate.py, entities.py, distill.py, embed.py, ingest.py, bin/claude-recall).

VAL-DB-009.details: Code inspection of src/memory_db.py confirms: migrate_schema() is defined at line 18, and get_conn() calls migrate_schema(conn) at line 65 (inside a try block that checks if the facts table exists). migrate_schema handles: (1) adding last_validated column to facts, (2) creating processed_messages table with index, (3) cleaning up entity_id=0 sentinels.

summary: Tested 9 assertions: all 9 passed. WAL mode verified on file-based DB (in-memory correctly returns 'memory'). All query functions (search_fts, search_facts_fts, get_session_messages, list_recent_sessions, store_fact) work correctly with in-memory test databases. No bare sqlite3.connect() calls found outside memory_db.py. migrate_schema() properly called from get_conn().
